data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-04-22T00:41:54.172Z</current_datetime>

I have an EF Core 8 app. These two operations are extremely slow because they load all entities into memory:

```csharp
// Operation 1: Deactivate all products not sold in 6 months
var staleProducts = await _db.Products
    .Where(p => p.LastSoldDate < DateTime.UtcNow.AddMonths(-6))
    .ToListAsync();
foreach (var p in staleProducts)
{
    p.IsActive = false;
}
await _db.SaveChangesAsync();

// Operation 2: Delete all audit logs older than 1 year
var oldLogs = await _db.AuditLogs
    .Where(l => l.CreatedAt < DateTime.UtcNow.AddYears(-1))
    .ToListAsync();
_db.AuditLogs.RemoveRange(oldLogs);
await _db.SaveChangesAsync();
```

There are 500K products and 2M audit logs. How do I make these efficient?

data.content:
Use EF Core 7+'s **bulk operations** — `ExecuteUpdateAsync` and `ExecuteDeleteAsync`. They translate directly to SQL, bypassing the change tracker entirely:

```csharp
// Operation 1: Bulk UPDATE — single SQL statement, zero entities loaded
await _db.Products
    .Where(p => p.LastSoldDate < DateTime.UtcNow.AddMonths(-6))
    .ExecuteUpdateAsync(s => s.SetProperty(p => p.IsActive, false));

// Operation 2: Bulk DELETE — single SQL statement, zero entities loaded
await _db.AuditLogs
    .Where(l => l.CreatedAt < DateTime.UtcNow.AddYears(-1))
    .ExecuteDeleteAsync();
```

**Key things to know:**

- These run **immediately** — no `SaveChangesAsync()` needed
- They **bypass the change tracker**, so any already-tracked entities will be stale
- They generate a single `UPDATE`/`DELETE` SQL statement
- For your volumes (500K products, 2M logs), this goes from minutes to seconds
- If you need to continue working with the DbContext after these calls, consider calling `_db.ChangeTracker.Clear()` to avoid stale data
data.reasoningText: The user wants to optimize two EF Core operations that currently load all matching entities into memory. In EF Core 7+, there are `ExecuteUpdateAsync` and `ExecuteDeleteAsync` methods that translate directly to SQL `UPDATE` and `DELETE` statements without loading entities.
